# Gulf Coast Clinical Consulting

> Legal nurse consulting firm founded by Dr. Kim D. Littles, DNP, MSN, RN-BC. We translate complex medical records into court-ready analysis for plaintiff and defense counsel, nationwide.

Gulf Coast Clinical Consulting is a legal nurse consulting practice based on the U.S. Gulf Coast and serving attorneys nationwide. The founder holds a Doctor of Nursing Practice degree, a Master of Science in Nursing, and Registered Nurse board certification. The practice reads clinical evidence precisely, plainly, and without opinion where opinion does not belong.

## Founder

Dr. Kim D. Littles, DNP, MSN, RN-BC — Legal Nurse Consultant.

Clinical specialties:
- Hematology / Oncology
- Immune Effector Cell Therapy
- Medical Surgical nursing

Credentials:
- DNP — Doctor of Nursing Practice
- MSN — Master of Science in Nursing
- RN-BC — Registered Nurse, Board Certified

## Services

- **Medical Record Review & Chronology** — organized timelines and record indexes counsel can hand to a jury.
- **Case Merit Screening** — early clinical read on whether a case has merit before you invest.
- **Standard-of-Care Analysis** — plain-language explanation of what the standard required and where care diverged.
- **Expert Witness Location & Preparation** — sourcing the right specialty expert and prepping them for deposition or trial.
- **Life Care Planning Support** — clinical framing for damages models.
- **Deposition & Trial Preparation** — question sets, hot-document packets, and clinical briefings for counsel.

## Case types

Medical malpractice, nursing home neglect and abuse, birth injury, personal injury, product liability, wrongful death, elder abuse, pharmaceutical liability. Any case that turns on nursing standards or clinical documentation.

## Who we work with

Plaintiff and defense counsel. Solo practitioners, small firms, and large firms. Gulf Coast (Texas, Louisiana, Mississippi, Alabama, Florida) and nationwide.
